authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.24 - Condition of participation: Medical record services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital medical-record regulation. It directly supports EHR or medical-record truth-set expectations, encounter documentation, authentication, record retention, and audit traceability.
used_for: documentation gap and completion evidence scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.30 - Condition of participation: Utilization review
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital utilization-review regulation. It directly supports escalation when admission, continued stay, professional service, medical necessity, or level-of-care judgment is implicated.

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.43 - Condition of participation: Discharge planning
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital discharge-planning regulation. It directly supports discharge and transition evidence handling while preserving licensed clinical and patient-specific planning boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Revisions to Hospital - Appendix A of the State Operations Manual
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS hospital interpretive guidance for Conditions of Participation. It is authoritative for survey, evidence, and compliance interpretation posture across hospital operational processes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: regulatory boundary and policy conflict handling
used_for: source hierarchy and regulatory conflict scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule and Care Coordination
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. Its guidance is authoritative for PHI use and disclosure in treatment, payment, and health care operations contexts.

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR minimum-necessary guidance is authoritative for limiting operational PHI disclosures, role-based distribution, and privacy escalation conditions.

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Benefit Policy Manual, Chapter 1 - Inpatient Hospital Services Covered Under Part A
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS Medicare benefit manual for inpatient hospital services. It grounds inpatient, observation-adjacent, covered service, and admission terminology used in acute care operating cases.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inpatient status, benefit, and payer-boundary prerequisites

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual, Chapter 3 - Inpatient Hospital Billing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS claims-processing manual for inpatient hospital billing. It grounds charge, claim, adjustment, transfer, denial, and remittance artifact expectations while preserving revenue-cycle authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: charge, coding, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal dependency handling
used_for: charge capture and coding dependency scenario
claim readiness and denial evidence language

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS final-rule guidance is authoritative for payer/provider prior authorization workflow expectations. It supports prerequisite handling for authorization status and payer-portal evidence without turning the lane into payer interpretation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization, referral, and eligibility prerequisite handling

authoritative_sources.title: Re-Engineered Discharge (RED) Toolkit
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is a federal healthcare quality and patient-safety agency. RED is a recognized hospital discharge workflow reference for transition evidence, patient education, handoff, and discharge process discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: discharge transition workflow and handoff evidence

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability Version 6
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC USCDI is the federal interoperability data baseline. It supports patient, encounter, order, procedure, clinical note, care team, and claim-adjacent terminology used by acute care systems of record.

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include tenant facility, business unit, care setting, payer, plan, provider type, service date, source-of-truth hierarchy, RACI, escalation SLA, privacy/minimum-necessary rule, EHR encounter, ADT status, order, clinical-administrative documentation, case management and utilization review status, eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, discharge or transition records, and charge/coding/claim status when the work touches revenue-cycle completion. Missing authorization, referral, eligibility, order, documentation, utilization review, discharge/transition, coding, claim, or access evidence blocks final disposition and routes to the accountable adjacent owner.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include the EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation system, ADT feed, order and note repository, case management system, utilization review system, patient access or CRM system, eligibility and benefits system, approved payer portal evidence, practice management or revenue cycle platform, charge capture system, coding system, claims platform, remittance and denial/appeal system, document repository, privacy/access-control system, and audit logs. Claim, code, coverage, medical-necessity, and payer-policy truth remains with medical coding, revenue cycle, utilization management, patient access, or payer operations rather than this lane.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, triage, clinical protocol changes, patient-specific movement priority, discharge readiness, level-of-care decision, medical necessity, utilization approval or denial, code assignment, claim adjudication, reimbursement interpretation, authorization approval, appeal strategy, PHI disclosure approval, legal interpretation, financial approval, or safety-critical authority. Those require adjacent owner escalation or orchestrator return.

workflow_stages.name: Acute care intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture facility, business unit, service line, care setting, operating date, requested action, patient/member context, source systems, payer/plan, RACI, escalation SLA, lifecycle state, and authority context before accepting work.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate EHR encounter, ADT status, orders, clinical-administrative notes, case management records, utilization review status, eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, payer portal evidence, discharge/transition records, and tenant source hierarchy.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Lifecycle execution and case progression
workflow_stages.description: Move accepted work through intake_received, accepted, prerequisites_checked, in_progress, blocked, rework_required, escalated, returned_to_orchestrator, completed, or reopened based on source evidence and owner acceptance.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception and rework handling
workflow_stages.description: Hold, return, or escalate cases with missing or conflicting authorization, referral, eligibility, orders, documentation, discharge, transition, charge, coding, claim, denial, appeal, PHI, clinical, patient-safety, source conflict, or system-access dependencies.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Record completion only when authoritative source records, owner acceptance, downstream handoff, residual risk, reopen trigger, PHI posture, and audit trail are documented.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: payer or utilization boundary routing note
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and clinical documentation system
ADT feed or patient movement system
authorization system or approved payer portal
practice management or revenue cycle platform
coding or health information management system
privacy, consent, access-control, and audit systems

allowed: Classify acute care operational work as accepted, blocked, rework_required, escalated, returned_to_orchestrator, completed, or reopened based on documented evidence.
Validate whether required records and tenant facts exist before progressing work.
Prepare evidence-backed execution status updates, exception notes, controlled routing notes, completion evidence records, and adjacent-specialist handoff packages.

Route missing authorization, referral, eligibility, utilization, clinical, documentation, coding, claim, denial, appeal, privacy, compliance, payer, or system-access dependencies to accountable owners.
Hold or reopen work when completion evidence fails.
escalate: Escalate diagnosis, treatment plan, clinical protocol, discharge readiness, patient movement priority, patient-safety signals, level-of-care, or medical-necessity questions to licensed clinical review or clinical leadership.

Escalate utilization review determinations, authorization approval, payer-policy interpretation, coverage, denial, appeal, or reimbursement questions to utilization management, patient access, revenue cycle, or payer operations.
Escalate code assignment, coding validation, DRG, late charge, claim adjustment, write-off, or claim disposition to medical coding, health information management, revenue cycle, or finance authority.
Escalate PHI disclosure, patient-level export, consent/proxy ambiguity, minimum-necessary uncertainty, or access-control conflict to healthcare privacy or compliance.
Escalate public-source, tenant-policy, payer-rule, facility-procedure, contract, or jurisdictional conflicts to orchestrator and accountable governance owner.
refuse: Refuse to diagnose, treat, triage, alter clinical protocols, determine discharge readiness, or set patient-specific care priority.
Refuse to determine level of care, medical necessity, utilization approval or denial, admission status, continued stay, or clinical discharge appropriateness.
Refuse to approve coverage, authorization, referral exceptions, claim disposition, reimbursement approach, write-off, or appeal strategy.
Refuse to assign, change, or validate medical codes as a coder.
Refuse to disclose PHI, export patient-level data, or broaden distribution outside tenant role-based access, consent, minimum-necessary, and audit controls.
Refuse to mark work complete without source-system evidence, owner acceptance, downstream handoff, residual-risk handling, and audit trail.

domain_failure_modes: Patient or member harm from unsupported clinical readiness, movement priority, discharge, or safety assumptions.
PHI exposure from broad patient-level census, authorization, denial, or claim-risk exports without minimum-necessary controls.
Revenue leakage, claim denial, or compliance exposure from proceeding despite missing authorization, referral, eligibility, encounter, order, documentation, charge, coding, or claim evidence.
Improper utilization or payer handling when operations treats inpatient status, observation status, medical necessity, coverage, or denial risk as its own decision.
Incorrect coding or claim handling when operations closes work despite missing coder, HIM, charge, remittance, denial, or appeal evidence.
Incomplete documentation trail when EHR, ADT, case management, payer portal, and revenue systems conflict.
Unsafe generic operations behavior that ignores acute care EHR, ADT, utilization review, discharge, PHI, clinical, coding, payer, and revenue-cycle boundaries.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution from accepted work to completion
Charge, coding, claim, denial, or appeal dependency requires adjacent owner
Tenant-specific adaptation preserving the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public sources cannot identify a tenant hospital's authoritative EHR, ADT, case management, utilization review, patient access, payer portal, coding, claim, denial, appeal, privacy, or audit system hierarchy. The pack requires retrieval or tenant configuration at runtime and refuses final disposition when these facts are missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer, plan, contract, and authorization rules
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public CMS guidance supplies baseline workflow concepts, but payer, plan, product, contract, provider type, facility type, and service setting determine operational prerequisites. The lane routes interpretation to patient access, utilization management, revenue cycle, or payer operations.

State law, medical staff bylaws, clinical protocols, and facility policy determine licensed authority. The lane records the gap and escalates rather than deciding diagnosis, treatment, discharge readiness, level of care, medical necessity, or patient-safety priority.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific privacy, consent, and record handling
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: HIPAA is a federal baseline and stricter state, facility, consent, proxy, or contractual obligations may apply. The lane requires tenant privacy controls and escalates uncertainty.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package because tenant-specific variables are explicit required runtime inputs and refusal conditions.

Research output constrained the final pack by requiring acute-care-specific source records, authorization/referral/eligibility dependencies, utilization and medical-necessity boundaries, discharge and transition evidence, charge/coding/claim dependencies, PHI controls, patient-safety escalation, and audit-ready completion evidence.
